Business/Financial Desk; Section C
ENRON'S MANY STRANDS: THE MANY STRANDS OF ENRON
By JEFFREY K. SMITH

11/04/2001
The New York Times
Page 1, Column 5
c. 2001 New York Times Company

WASHINGTON -- Enron, the energy trading giant, has many loyalists, but few as loyal as its chief executive, Kenneth L. Lay.

Mr. Lay, who has been with Enron since its founding in 1985, has been a major financial backer of Republican causes and candidates, and he has been a generous contributor to the Bush campaign.

But Mr. Lay's most important role in Enron has been as a political force in Washington, where he has been a key player in shaping the energy policy of President Bush.

Mr. Lay has been a major force behind the Bush administration's energy policy, which has been characterized by a heavy reliance on natural gas and a heavy emphasis on deregulation.
